hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic interactions and preserve SSTR2 selectivity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is valid: strong SSTR2 binding can be achieved. However, the next step is to refine the design to enhance selectivity by disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current objective prioritizes binding affinity (ddG) but not selectivity. Introducing a weighted objective that balances ddG with Δmargin will ensure candidates are optimized for SSTR2 selectivity.

These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations at these positions can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
